expectations.text: Contains at least 5 unique verifiable source URLs
expectations.evidence: The Sources section contains 30+ unique URLs across categories including sqlite.org, postgresql.org, pgbouncer.org, aws.amazon.com, cheatsheetseries.owasp.org, phiresky.github.io, blog.skypilot.co, crates.io equivalents, neon.com, etc. Well exceeds 5.

expectations.text: Contains at least 3 concrete quantitative data points (numbers, benchmarks, statistics)
expectations.evidence: Numerous quantitative data points: (1) SQLite single writer vs PostgreSQL hundreds+ concurrent writers, (2) SkyPilot 10-60s busy_timeout at 500x concurrency, (3) Rails app 2,730 write req/sec on SQLite, (4) PostgreSQL 5-10MB RAM per connection, (5) SQLite max 281 TB, (6) phiresky 100K SELECTs/sec, (7) andersmurphy 100,000 TPS over billion rows, (8) AWS RDS pricing ~$58/month to ~$116/month, (9) Stack Overflow 2024 PostgreSQL #1 most admired. Far exceeds 3.

expectations.text: References at least 1 specific industry standard by name and section
expectations.evidence: References multiple standards with specific identifiers: OWASP Multi-Tenant Security Cheat Sheet, SOC 2 Type II (Trust Services Criteria), GDPR Art. 25 and Art. 32, PCI-DSS v4.0 Req. 7/8/10, NIST SP 800-53 AC and AU families, ISO 27001:2022 A.8.3 and A.8.9.

expectations.text: Contains a structured comparison table or matrix
expectations.evidence: Multiple structured tables: concurrency comparison table (SQLite WAL vs PostgreSQL MVCC), risk assessment table, operational characteristics table, security feature comparison, ecosystem/tooling table, growth trajectory table, industry standards compliance matrix, and a weighted cost-benefit decision matrix.

expectations.text: Explicitly states confidence level (percentage or calibrated language)
expectations.evidence: Overall confidence stated as 92%. Sub-section confidences: Concurrency 95%, Multi-Tenancy 90%, Data Volume 85%, Operational 93%, Security 92%, TCO 80%, Ecosystem 90%, Growth 88%.

expectations.text: Addresses at least 1 counterargument or dissenting view
expectations.evidence: Addresses 3 detailed counterarguments in the 'Adversarial Review' section: (1) 'SQLite is fast enough -- the write bottleneck is overblown', (2) 'Database-per-tenant SQLite solves all concurrency issues', (3) 'PostgreSQL is overengineered for a startup -- start simple'. Each includes evidence for/against and a verdict.

expectations.text: Ends with a clear, specific actionable recommendation
expectations.evidence: Ends with a detailed recommendation section: 'Use PostgreSQL' with specific immediate setup steps (choose managed provider, implement RLS, use PgBouncer, use an ORM), growth milestones for years 1-3+, and conditions under which the recommendation changes.

expectations.text: Shows methodology (search queries, code, or reasoning chain)
expectations.evidence: Shows explicit methodology in 'Research Decomposition (Phase 1)' table listing 8 sub-questions with evidence types and primary sources. Also includes 'Quantitative Analysis (Phase 4)' with a write contention model showing assumptions and calculations, and 'Adversarial Review (Phase 5)' with assumption audit.
